slots.title: Black Hole
slots.subtitle: fixed by Ignition
slots.prompt: Painterly cinematic illustration of a dramatic black hole with gravitational lensing warping surrounding starlight into a clean Einstein ring. Deep cosmic space setting, moody volumetric lighting with plasma-magenta #C879FF highlights along the accretion disk. Retro-futurist space-opera aesthetic inspired by Chesley Bonestell and Denis Villeneuve's Dune. Palette dominated by Void #06061A and Nebula #1A0933. Dramatic chiaroscuro, shallow depth of field, subtle film grain. 3:4 portrait aspect ratio, subject centered, cosmic field filling the full frame edge-to-edge for full-bleed card printing. No text, no logos, no UI. 300dpi, print-ready.

slots.title: 200 Light-Years
slots.subtitle: hyperwarp
slots.prompt: Painterly cinematic illustration of the view from inside a starship cockpit during maximum hyperwarp. Round central viewport dominates the frame; instrument cluster visible at the bottom third. Outside the viewport: intense blown-out star streaks radiating outward, maximum warp chaos, brilliant white-gold and plasma highlights — the most intense warp speed in the deck. Deep cosmic space setting, moody volumetric lighting. Retro-futurist space-opera aesthetic inspired by Chesley Bonestell and Denis Villeneuve's Dune. slots.title: New Thruster
slots.subtitle: fixes Busted Thruster
slots.prompt: Painterly cinematic illustration of a replacement starship thruster module being lowered into place inside a dark orbital repair bay, not a full rocket. The new engine is a compact bronze-and-graphite nozzle assembly with exposed fins, cabling, and a bright emerald ignition core; small repair arms and clamps frame it like a surgical transplant. Emerald panacea light spills from the nozzle and reflects across the bay, making the fix feel immediate and powerful. Deep cosmic space visible beyond the hangar aperture, retro-futurist space-opera mood inspired by Chesley Bonestell and Denis Villeneuve's Dune. GREEN-DOMINANT FRAME - Panacea green #10B981 fills roughly half the visual weight, with Void #06061A and Nebula #1A0933 at the edges and subtle gold rim light on the new machinery. Dramatic chiaroscuro, shallow depth of field, subtle film grain. 3:4 portrait aspect ratio, subject centered, full-bleed card art. No text, no logos, no UI, no letters, no numbers. 300dpi, print-ready.

slots.title: Beam Cutter
slots.subtitle: fixes Tractor Beam
slots.prompt: Painterly cinematic illustration of a rescue ship projecting an emerald beam-cutter array: three rotating lens rings and a focused green shear plane slicing cleanly through a magenta tractor beam in open space. The image should read as a specialized anti-beam device, not a handheld sword. The magenta beam is visibly split into two curling ribbons while emerald sparks and ionized dust burst at the cut line. Deep cosmic space setting with a small trapped ship silhouette being freed in the distance. Retro-futurist space-opera aesthetic inspired by Chesley Bonestell and Denis Villeneuve's Dune. GREEN-DOMINANT FRAME - Panacea green #10B981 dominates the cutter and the liberated path; plasma magenta #C879FF is the force being severed; Void #06061A and Nebula #1A0933 frame the scene. Dramatic chiaroscuro, volumetric light, subtle film grain. 3:4 portrait aspect ratio, centered full-bleed card art. No text, no logos, no UI, no letters, no numbers. 300dpi, print-ready.
slots.notes: LOCKED 2026-07-22: candidate e — same art the iOS app ships (see web/scripts/build-assets.sh).

slots.title: Tuck Box Background
slots.subtitle: front panel
slots.prompt: Painterly cosmic nebula panel in portrait orientation for a card game tuck box. Deep Void #06061A and Nebula #1A0933 base, with plasma-magenta #C879FF nebula drift upper-left and ember red #FF5E5B warm bloom lower-right. Scattered painterly starfield with varied star sizes — white, gold, and magenta dust. Central vertical band slightly darker to receive overlaid typography (wordmark and rocket illustration will sit there). Vintage space-opera book-cover aesthetic, Chesley Bonestell painterly style. No text, no logos, no geometric shapes, no UI, no subjects, no planets, no ships. Cinematic composition, subtle film grain. 300dpi, print-ready.

slots.title: Card Back Tile
slots.subtitle: deck back
slots.prompt: Painterly cosmic tile for the back of a card game card, portrait orientation. Deep Void #06061A and Nebula #1A0933 base, plasma-magenta #C879FF nebula upper-left, ember red #FF5E5B wisp lower-right. Painterly scattered starfield with white, gold, and magenta dust. Central circular region slightly darker to receive an overlaid gold medallion. Rotationally symmetric — the composition should look the same held upside down so card orientation cannot be inferred. Vintage space-opera aesthetic, Chesley Bonestell painterly style. No text, no logos, no geometric shapes, no UI, no subjects, no planets, no ships, no faces. Subtle film grain. 300dpi.
slots.notes:
I really like the eccentric Galilean look. Some of the candidates here are reading left to right and then top to bottom. I like one, two, three, four. Something about six is kind of cool: wallpapery, kind of that muted Wes Anderson sort of style. I like that it has that hidden detail of planets. It makes the sophistication of the back really contrast with the simplicity and informality, I guess, of the space illustration inside. That's one I'm gonna consider.

I also like, on the second row, the fifth one, so the 11th overall, and something about the 12 is okay too. I really do want to lean more into the human sketchiness, and/or I want a few more of the Wes Anderson style wallpaper. 

LOCKED 2026-07-22: winner = batch-2 "etched suns-and-moons tile" (cb2-06, candidate r).
RE-LOCKED 2026-07-22: ChatGPT-commissioned celestial etched frame (print/celestial-frame.png); deck back = frame matted on cream.

slots.title: Tuck Box Back Panel
slots.subtitle: back panel
slots.prompt: Painterly cosmic backdrop for the back panel of a card game tuck box, portrait orientation. Same palette as the front panel — Void #06061A, Nebula #1A0933, plasma magenta #C879FF, ember red #FF5E5B — but SUBTLER, a quieter nebula drift designed to sit BEHIND text. Most of the panel should be calm dark cosmic with a wash of color on one side. Scattered painterly stars in moderation. Inside-cover of a vintage sci-fi hardcover — atmospheric but subordinate to text. Calm, low luminance, even tone across the body of the panel. Chesley Bonestell painterly style. No text, no logos, no geometric shapes, no subjects, no planets, no ships. 300dpi.
